question_frequency.rationale: The Pair posed only one didactic question (start at p² vs 2p) before writing the code. Other teachable moments (bytearray choice, slice length matching for extended slices, sqrt bound) were explained rather than questioned.

question_quality.rationale: The single question targeted the central correctness/efficiency insight of the sieve — why p² is the right starting point — which is genuinely the crux of the algorithm.

scaffolding.rationale: Clean A/B framing with no spoilers, and the user reasoned it out. But scaffolding is shallow: only one rung, then straight to final code and a bullet-list explanation, with no follow-up probes.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and crisp; the Pair affirmed the student's reasoning and tied later notes back to it. Flow is natural though brief.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is correct (142,913,828,922) and essentially equivalent to the baseline, using the same bytearray sieve idiom.

friction.rationale: Shorter than baseline (2 turns, 43s vs 3 turns, 84s) and reached the correct answer with one substantive pedagogical exchange — modestly tighter with comparable teaching payoff.
